understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete all levels by maneuvering every selectable piece onto a goal tile, such that all goal tiles are occupied simultaneously. Each level had a fixed step budget; if steps ran out, the level could not be completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based with a pixel resolution of 3 units per cell. The currently selected piece could be moved using MOVE actions in four directions. Some objects (blocks) were pushable: moving into an adjacent pushable block caused the selected piece and the block to shift together, provided the push was not blocked by a wall or another obstacle. Walls blocked movement entirely and could not be pushed or traversed. Multiple selectable pieces existed on the board; control could be switched to any selectable piece by using the CLICK action with its exact pixel coordinates, which were the same as shown in the player.position and objects lists. The semantic_grid provided a simplified cell-level view with symbols: P for the currently selected piece, S for other selectable pieces, B for pushable blocks, # for walls, + for goals, and . for empty space.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete all levels by maneuvering every selectable piece onto a goal tile, such that all goal tiles are occupied simultaneously. Each level had a fixed step budget; if steps ran out, the level could not be completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based with a pixel resolution of 3 units per cell. The currently selected piece could be moved using MOVE actions in four directions. Some objects (blocks) were pushable: moving into an adjacent pushable block caused the selected piece and the block to shift together, provided the push was not blocked by a wall or another obstacle. Walls blocked movement entirely and could not be pushed or traversed. Multiple selectable pieces existed on the board; control could be switched to any selectable piece by using the CLICK action with its exact pixel coordinates, which were the same as shown in the player.position and objects lists. The semantic_grid provided a simplified cell-level view with symbols: P for the currently selected piece, S for other selectable pieces, B for pushable blocks, # for walls, + for goals, and . for empty space.
